title: hypericum henryi subsp. henryi
intro: Hypericum henryi subsp. henryi, a subspecies of flowering plant, is a member of the St. John's Wort family (Hypericaceae) and is native to China and Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antiseptic activities. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and depression, and has been used to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The leaves and flowers of Hypericum henryi subsp. henryi can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
